key_items: Bitcoin worst week since FTX with ~20% single-week decline; realized losses still $35B below 2022 total suggesting deeper bear market ahead
Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash (RSI metrics) with $2.6B short trap potentially brewing, but mining cost floor at $60K may mark cycle bottom
Macro headwinds: $900B Treasury cash rebuild could drain liquidity, Federal Reserve rate-cut bets evaporating, CLARITY Act passage odds fallen to 60%
reasoning: Bitcoin shows extreme oversold technicals (RSI mirrors 2020/Feb 2026 setups) suggesting potential 30-50% rebounds to $70K, creating a mixed short-term bounce setup; however, fundamental bearish pressures dominate: realized losses trajectory points to lower lows ahead, macro liquidity conditions deteriorating, regulatory uncertainty increasing, and institutional adoption narrative being tested by the pullback.
risks: Treasury's $900B cash rebuild combined with evaporating rate-cut expectations could trigger sustained liquidity drain, pushing BTC below the $60K mining cost support floor toward the $50K bear market target cited by analysts.
